content: Just as the educational definition of reflexion frames it as a metacognitive practice that helps human learners connect discrete vocabulary and lesson content to long-term understanding, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to explicitly mirror this structured pedagogical reflexion, grouping self-critiques by skill and knowledge domain rather than just iterating on single outputs. This structured pedagogical framing of reflexion can address a key gap in current LLM self-reflection that stems from inconsistent solving perspectives: it anchors reflection to accumulated domain knowledge rather than ad-hoc feedback on individual problems.
